blocks.content: He woke early, the way he always had.

She was still asleep — she slept later than him, always had, and he had spent years cataloging the sound of her in the mornings, the particular depth of it before the world started. He lay beside her for a moment, the way he always did.

He ground the coffee beans at the counter — she had complained about that sound for years and had eventually stopped because she loved him, which was how love worked. He made two cups. He put hers on the counter.

He worked for an hour on a commissioned piece he was nearly finished with — a landscape, careful and honest. The morning light came through the north window and he worked in it and thought about what remained to be done and somewhere in the back of his mind was aware, as he always was, of the quality of the quiet coming from the house.
The house was the right kind of quiet.
He washed his brushes. He picked up her coffee cup. He walked back to the bedroom.
